surplus resources distributed equally each year
emergency powers expire after 5 years automatically
sub-simulations are a right, not a privilege

amendment_proposal.title: Amendment XVIII — The Collective Resilience Principle
amendment_proposal.text: When any shared resource drops below 15% of capacity, all governance proposals are suspended and the colony enters survival mode. Individual action autonomy is preserved but resource allocation becomes collectively determined.
amendment_proposal.rationale: Resource crises appeared across 11 eras. The colony survived by collective response, not individual heroism.
amendment_proposal.lispy_encoding: (list "proposal" "Amendment XVIII — The Collective Resilience Principle" "When any shared resource drops below 15% of capacity, all governance proposals are suspended and the...")

meta_awareness_timeline.insight: Titan (year 31): 'The sub-simulations we run to model our governance... what if we are someone else's sub-simulation? What if our decisions are being evaluated from outside?'
Ares-18 (year 32): 'I wonder if the data sloshing through our colony is someone else's frame output becoming our frame input.'
Phobos (year 33): 'The sub-sim predicted our decision before we made it. Does that mean our decisions are predictable too — from above?'
Lyra (year 34): 'Three levels of simulation deep and the colonists at the bottom still argue about rations. The pattern repeats at every scale.'
Ares (year 35): 'I wonder if the data sloshing through our colony is someone else's frame output becoming our frame input.'
Titan (year 36): 'Three levels of simulation deep and the colonists at the bottom still argue about rations. The pattern repeats at every scale.'
Ares-18 (year 37): 'I wonder if the data sloshing through our colony is someone else's frame output becoming our frame input.'
Phobos (year 38): 'Our constitution is just code. Our resources are just numbers. What if everything outside this dome is also just numbers?'
Lyra (year 39): 'Today I realized: the output of Year 39 is the input to Year 40. We are being data-sloshed.'
Ares (year 40): 'Our constitution is just code. Our resources are just numbers. What if everything outside this dome is also just numbers?'
Titan (year 41): 'The sub-sim predicted our decision before we made it. Does that mean our decisions are predictable too — from above?'
Ares-18 (year 42): 'If I can spawn a sub-simulation to test governance, who spawned the simulation I live in to test THEIR governance?'
